humanities_role_explanation: Uses nearly a century (1939-2020s) of U.S. Treasury Bulletins as the document corpus, which serves as historical financial and economic data for factual QA items, connecting directly to modern economic history.
benchmark_modalities_explanation: Primarily relies on textual and tabular documents, but 3% of the questions require visual reasoning over figures, charts, or graphs present in the PDF bulletins.
analytical_tasks_explanation: Requires multi-step analytical reasoning across unstructured text and complex tables, including longitudinal financial analysis (e.g., ordinary least squares linear regression), inflation adjustment calculations, and high-precision numerical operations.

general_purpose_benchmark.note: Specifically designed as an enterprise grounded reasoning benchmark focused on financial and historical document QA.

evaluates_generative_llm.note: Evaluates frontier LLMs and agent frameworks including Claude Opus 4.6/4.5, Claude Sonnet 4.6/4.5, Claude Haiku 4.5, GPT-5.4, GPT-5.1, GPT-5 Mini, Gemini 3.1 Pro Preview, Gemini 3 Pro Preview, and Gemini 3 Flash.

llm_is_primary_system.note: The LLMs and LLM-based agents are the primary systems being evaluated and benchmarked.

arts_humanities_data.note: Includes 'History' as it utilizes a 100-year historical archive of U.S. Treasury Bulletins spanning from 1939 onwards.

quantitative_metrics.note: Evaluated using accuracy under varying absolute relative error thresholds (0.0%, 0.1%, 1.0%, 5.0%), exact match, and fuzzy text matching.

english_language.note: The paper, corpus, and questions are written in English.

summary: OfficeQA Pro is an enterprise benchmark designed to evaluate AI agents on end-to-end grounded reasoning over a heterogeneous 100-year archive of U.S. Treasury Bulletins. It contains 133 highly complex and verifiable questions requiring precise document parsing, information retrieval, and multi-step quantitative data analysis. The benchmark unifies parsing, retrieval, and computation to measure SOTA model limitations in economically valuable enterprise tasks.
key_findings: Frontier agents struggle on over half of the questions, scoring 34.1% on average on the full PDF corpus. Using Databricks' ai_parse_document for structured parsing yields a 16.1% relative performance gain. Claude Opus 4.6 achieves the highest accuracy at 57.1% when utilizing file search over pre-parsed documents, while direct LLM prompt-only baseline scores <3% accuracy.
